title: phyllanthus bathianus
intro: Phyllanthus bathianus, also known as the West Indian gooseberry or the West Indian cherry, is a shrub-like plant native to tropical regions of the Caribbean and Central America. It is a member of the Phyllanthaceae family and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of P. bathianus include its potential to protect against liver damage, lower blood pressure, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. Herbal preparations of P. bathianus can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and capsules, and are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
